checks.value: verify that calling spectraData() on a MsBackendTest instance with only user-supplied spectra variables (msLevel, rtime) returns a DataFrame
verify that the returned DataFrame contains all core spectra variables as columns
verify that columns corresponding to missing core spectra variables (e.g., centroided, polarity) contain NA values
verify that fillCoreSpectraVariables() is invoked during spectraData() execution by inspecting function call stack or code inspection

direct_checks: verify that calling spectraData() on a MsBackendTest instance with only user-supplied spectra variables (msLevel, rtime) returns a DataFrame
verify that the returned DataFrame contains all core spectra variables as columns
verify that columns corresponding to missing core spectra variables (e.g., centroided, polarity) contain NA values
verify that fillCoreSpectraVariables() is invoked during spectraData() execution by inspecting function call stack or code inspection
expert_review: expert review of whether the set of core spectra variables returned matches the documented specification for core variables
expert review of whether NA-filling behavior is appropriate and consistent with the MsBackend API contract

review_questions: Is the research question correctly identified and scoped?
Does the connected finding have enough supporting evidence?
Which artifacts are required before this can become an executable benchmark task?
What direct, visual, textual, or expert-review checks should be used for evaluation?
